text: Data collected in experimental studies in Rainbow Trout found that AChE inhibition decreased heart rate and increased ventilation volume. However, in spite of the increase in ventilation volume, oxygen utilization remained low. Therefore, it is biologically plausible that reduced fluid flow through the gills leads to respiratory distress in fish (Duangsawasdi, 1977).      Acephate and fenitrothion exposure in adult rainbow trout resulted in decreased heart rate, increased ventilation rate and amplitude similarly characteristic to fish exposed to hypoxia. ECG after exposure showed irregular heart beats and changes in ECG waveforms (Duagsawasdi 1978, Duagsawasdi and Klaverkamp, 1979).
